subject: UX & Research
title: Siri and the Voice Interface
description: A quick win: understand Siri and the Voice Interface.

hook.text: In 2011, Apple launched Siri. For the first time, millions of people talked to their phones and got answers.

buildup.text: Siri started as a DARPA-funded AI project at SRI International. Apple acquired it in 2010 for $200 million.

discovery.text: Voice interfaces eliminated the screen entirely for simple tasks: set a timer, send a text, check the weather.

twist.text: But voice failed at complex tasks. Users couldn't browse, compare, or scan results by talking. Vision is faster.

climax.text: Voice is great for hands-free, eyes-free moments. It complements screens but doesn't replace them.

punchline.text: Voice works for simple commands. Screens win for complex tasks.

quiz.question: Why hasn't voice fully replaced screen-based interfaces?
options: Complex tasks like browsing and comparing require visual scanning that voice can't match
Voice recognition technology isn't accurate enough
Users prefer typing over speaking in all situations
